steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: Before the blank, we have an independent clause (“The clashing notes can echo a long way across the mountains”) followed by “which is why” — a relative clause that introduces a causal explanation. The blank must contain a clause that provides the logical reason.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause rule
steps.content:
A relative clause introduced by “which is why” must use normal word order (subject before auxiliary verb) and end with a period that closes the sentence.

The wording “ganja has been used as a communication method.” correctly applies this rule with normal word order and appropriate closing punctuation.

“Ganja has been used as a communication method?” misplaces a question mark, and the versions beginning “has ganga” invert the word order unnecessarily.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
